purpose: Local plugin manifest schema v0.3: tightens hook lifecycle vocabulary and required lifecycle read/policy permission to v1-only contract.
note: Iterates on local 0.2 by narrowing hooks[].name to the v1 HookKind set ('before_invocation', 'after_invocation', 'on_error', 'on_cancel') and requiring each v0.3 hook permission list to contain plugin:lifecycle:read or plugin:lifecycle:policy. Lifecycle hook permissions must be required top-level permissions for the firewall trust gate; fail_closed lifecycle hooks must additionally declare plugin:lifecycle:policy. after_invocation, on_error, and on_cancel remain fail_open-only so terminal observability cannot mask the original completion, error, or cancellation cause. Deferred and excluded hook names remain accepted only by the still-supported v0.2 schema for backward compatibility. Minimal v0.3 lifecycle dispatch covers before_invocation/after_invocation plus the terminal observability hooks promoted by Wave 1 PR E.

plugin.schema.json: Narrowed hooks[].name enum to the v1 HookKind set: 'before_invocation', 'after_invocation', and the additive terminal observability hooks 'on_error' / 'on_cancel' promoted from the deferred bucket by Wave 1 PR E (#1131, refs #939 scope decision). Remaining deferred candidates (before_tool_call, after_tool_call, before_artifact_write, after_artifact_write) and excluded candidates (before_runtime_start, after_runtime_start, before_state_commit, after_state_commit, on_event, on_rewind) are still rejected at the schema layer; refer to src/ouroboros/plugin/hooks.py for the routing helpers and follow-up RFC slices.

Constrains 'after_invocation', 'on_error', and 'on_cancel' hooks to failure_policy='fail_open' at the schema layer so terminal observability hooks cannot mask the original error/cancel cause through a fail_closed veto.

Requires hooks[].permissions and requires that array to contain plugin:lifecycle:read or plugin:lifecycle:policy so archived v0.3 JSON Schema and Python loader enforce the same lifecycle permission boundary; lifecycle scopes used by hooks must be required top-level permissions, and fail_closed hooks additionally require plugin:lifecycle:policy as a required top-level permission in the Python loader.

Allows plugin.hook.blocked / plugin.hook.failed / plugin.hook.invoked / plugin.hook.completed in explicit audit.events declarations; explicit v0.3 audit.events must include the complete runtime-emitted vocabulary for the manifest shape, including core command events and hook events when hooks are declared.
audit-event.schema.json: Vendored audit-event.schema.json with local 0.3 $id/title/schema_version alignment and complete v1 hook wrapper event names: plugin.hook.invoked, plugin.hook.completed, plugin.hook.blocked, and plugin.hook.failed.

Confirmed plugin.hook.invoked / plugin.hook.completed as schema-vendored runtime event names for the v1 minimal lifecycle wrapper; v0.3 after_invocation hooks are schema-gated to fail_open because lifecycle observation must not veto completed invocations.
